cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
cero: noun. A large scombroid food fish (Scomberomorus regalis) found chiefly in the West Indies.
come: verb. To move nearer to the point of perspective.
comer: noun. One in a race who is catching up to others and shows promise of winning.
coo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Comox.
cooee: noun. A long, loud call used to attract attention when at a distance, mainly done in the Australian bush.
cor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Cornish.
core: noun. In general usage, an essential part of a thing surrounded by other essential things.
corm: noun. A short, vertical, swollen, underground stem of a plant (usually one of the monocots) that serves as a storage organ to enable the
corvee: noun. Unpaid labor required by a feudal lord.
cove: noun. A hollow in a rock; a cave or cavern.
eme: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Emerillon.
eve: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Even.
mere: adjective. Just, only; no more than, pure and simple, neither more nor better than might be expected.
moc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mocoví.
moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
moor: noun. An extensive waste covered with patches of heath, and having a poor, light (and usually acidic) soil, but sometimes marshy, and
mor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Moro.
more: determiner. comparative degree of many: in greater number.
move: verb. To change place or posture; to go, in any manner, from one place or position to another.
mover: noun. Someone who or something that moves.
omer: noun. A former small Hebrew unit of dry volume equal to about 2.3 L or 2.1 quarts.
orc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orma.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
overcome: verb. To surmount (a physical or abstract obstacle); to prevail over, to get the better of.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
rem: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Remo.
rev: verb. To increase the speed of a motor, or to operate at a higher speed.
roc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cacgia Roglai.
roe: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ronji.
rom: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Romani.
romeo: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Romeo of the ICAO / NATO radiotelephony alphabet.
room: noun. An opportunity or scope (to do something).
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
vomer: noun. The vomer bone; the small thin bone that forms part of the septum between the nostrils.
vroom: interjection. The sound of an engine, especially when it is revving up.
